author: chatgpt-codex-connector
body:
**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Size and protect the servo supply for stall current**

For this exact six-servo arm, the dossier later cites ~2.5 A stall current per MG996R-class servo, so the suggested "5V 10A class supply for six servos" is undersized when several joints hit an end stop or lift load at once. That makes the top-10 claim that the supply "prevents" brownout too strong for the failure mode calibration is meant to avoid; add explicit headroom/fusing/current limiting or an e-stop/power-switch recommendation rather than pointing a beginner at a generic 10 A brick.

Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.
